access.user: Can read public schedule information. Cannot modify schedule fact data.
access.admin: Can read public schedule information. Does not directly modify schedule facts through ordinary pages.
access.agent: Can read schedules for specific sections; current-user schedule aggregation is primarily exposed via MCP; public schedule retrieval is available through REST and MCP.

rules.schedule-definition: Schedule refers to a section's class times, locations, and week patterns.
rules.schedule-as-context: The schedule within a section detail page is not treated as an isolated table but as an important entry point for understanding the section context.
rules.personal-calendar-integrates: The personal calendar integrates subscribed section schedules, homework due dates, exam times, and personal todo due dates.

notes: Resolves the current user's subscribed sections server-side.
Supports dateFrom/dateTo, weekday, limit, and locale filters.
weekday accepts 1-7 and limit accepts 1-300 on both REST and MCP.
Use /api/schedules for public schedule queries without personal subscription scope.

fields.name: schedules
notes: DateTime range inputs normalize to Asia/Shanghai calendar dates for the Schedule.date @db.Date column.
Each Schedule.teachers(page: PageInput) field returns TeacherPage! with the shared default page 1/pageSize 20 and maximum pageSize 100.

tools.name: list_my_schedules
notes: Returns schedules[]; each item contains date, weekday, startTime, endTime, section.course.namePrimary, section.code, room, teachers[], and scheduleGroup.
weekday accepts 1-7 and limit accepts 1-300 on both REST and MCP.
Omitting semesterId reads all followed semesters; pass a semesterId to recover one current or past term.
Supports filtering by section, teacher, room, date range, and day of week.
REST/MCP both accept exact JW/code-style filters for schedules so callers do not need to resolve internal numeric IDs first.
REST pageSize accepts 1-100; the deprecated limit alias remains accepted and pageSize wins when both are supplied. Out-of-range values are rejected instead of clamped.
REST localizes nested schedule labels from the request locale; MCP accepts an explicit locale input.

tools.name: query_schedules
notes: MCP limit accepts 1-100 and rejects out-of-range values instead of clamping.
Returns a simplified variant of ScheduleEntry[] for the section.
Accepts optional dateFrom/dateTo and limit query parameters to narrow results to the same window supported by list_schedules_by_section.
REST localizes nested schedule labels from the request locale; MCP accepts an explicit locale input.
Returns schedule groups with schedules, not separate aggregated period summaries.
REST localizes nested schedule labels from the request locale; MCP accepts an explicit locale input.

tools.name: list_schedules_by_section
tools.returns: { found: Boolean, section: Section?, schedules: ScheduleEntry[] }
notes: Default and the deprecated summary alias omit the repeated per-item section payload because the top-level section already scopes the list.
Accepts optional dateFrom/dateTo to narrow results to a specific window (e.g. the current week); both accept flexible date strings.

tools.name: get_my_7days_timeline
tools.returns: { range: { from: DateTime, to: DateTime }, total: Int, events: CalendarEvent[] }
notes: Returns schedules as the schedule type within a unified event stream.
The deprecated summary input returns the same compact events[] structure as default instead of changing the collection type.
Accepts optional atTime to anchor the 7-day window to a specific moment instead of the server clock; useful for asking about a future week.
